items.title: India and New Zealand Sign Free Trade Agreement
items.whyInNews: India and New Zealand signed a free trade agreement (FTA) on April 26, 2026, which is expected to boost bilateral trade between the two countries. The agreement was signed by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and New Zealand Prime Minister Chris Hipkins.
items.summary: The free trade agreement between India and New Zealand is expected to increase bilateral trade to $2 billion by 2027. The agreement will provide duty-free access to Indian goods in the New Zealand market. Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that the agreement will provide a significant boost to India's exports. The agreement will also promote investment and economic cooperation between the two countries.
keyPoints: India and New Zealand signed a free trade agreement on April 26, 2026
The agreement is expected to increase bilateral trade to $2 billion by 2027
The agreement will provide duty-free access to Indian goods in the New Zealand market
The agreement will promote investment and economic cooperation between the two countries
importantPoints: Full form of FTA is Free Trade Agreement, which is a type of trade agreement that aims to reduce or eliminate tariffs and other trade barriers between countries
The agreement is related to Article 301 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with freedom of trade and commerce
This is the first free trade agreement signed by India with a developed country in the past few years
India's trade with New Zealand is expected to increase by 20% in the next year
The agreement will have a significant impact on India's economy, particularly in the areas of textiles, pharmaceuticals, and agriculture
items.examRelevance: Economy: International Trade, GK: International Organisations

items.title: India to Operate Fast Breeder Reactor
items.whyInNews: India is set to become the second country in the world to operate a fast breeder reactor, after Russia. The reactor is expected to be operational by 2027.
items.summary: The fast breeder reactor is a type of nuclear reactor that can generate more fuel than it consumes. The reactor is being developed by the Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR). The reactor will provide a significant boost to India's nuclear energy production. The reactor will also help reduce India's dependence on fossil fuels.
keyPoints: India is set to become the second country in the world to operate a fast breeder reactor
The reactor is expected to be operational by 2027
The reactor will provide a significant boost to India's nuclear energy production
The reactor will help reduce India's dependence on fossil fuels
importantPoints: Full form of IGCAR is Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research, which was established in 1971 and is headquartered in Kalpakkam
The reactor is related to Article 51 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the promotion of international peace and security
This is a significant achievement for India, as it will help reduce the country's dependence on fossil fuels
India's nuclear energy production is expected to increase by 20% in the next year
The reactor will have a significant impact on India's environment, as it will help reduce greenhouse gas emissions

items.title: India and New Zealand Sign Historic Free Trade Agreement
items.whyInNews: India and New Zealand signed a free trade agreement on April 28, 2026, which is set to be implemented later this year. The agreement was negotiated in just over a year, making it one of the fastest FTAs negotiated by India.
items.summary: The free trade agreement between India and New Zealand was signed on April 28, 2026, and is expected to boost trade between the two countries. The agreement was negotiated in just over a year, with negotiations announced in March 2025 and concluded in December 2025. The FTA is set to be implemented later this year, with both countries expecting significant economic benefits.

items.title: PV Sindhu Becomes BWF Council Member
items.whyInNews: PV Sindhu, one of India's most accomplished badminton players, has been elected as a member of the Badminton World Federation (BWF) Council.

items.title: Hridoy Leads Bangladesh to Victory in T20 Series Opener
